annotations.append_text: — Note: Contradiction score 1.0 is a scoring artifact. The 10 wicks breaking at $34-$35 zone (Sep 2025–Feb 2026) CONFIRM this lesson's claim about POC zone unreliability; they do not contradict it.

— Note: Contradiction score 0.89 is a scoring artifact. The 7 wicks breaking in the $6.24-$6.99 range (Sep 2025–Feb 2026) CONFIRM this lesson's warning about $7.01 level unreliability; they do not contradict it.

portfolio_lessons.text: Wick offset strategy is structurally validated: break rates are 66-72% across ALL approach-count tiers (1-3: 72%, 4-6: 66%, 7+: 71%) based on 2,664 total approaches. Entry prices must always be set below raw support using wick_offset_analyzer — placing limits at raw support levels violates a data-validated rule.

Technology sector has 100% win rate across 10 completed trades (CIFR ×4, CLSK ×3, BBAI, SOUN, SMCI, TEM). When surgical screening produces comparable scores across sectors, prioritize tech candidates.
Industrial sector confirms strategy portability: 5/5 trades profitable. The wick-offset entry framework generalizes beyond pure tech to space, defense, and industrial-adjacent sectors.

Approach count does not predict level reliability. Break rates are roughly equal at 66-72% regardless of whether a level has 1-3, 4-6, or 7+ historical approaches. Level reliability is determined by hold rate (percentage held), not raw approach count. Only levels with 75%+ hold rate should be treated as high-confidence.
